observations: Compliance depends on the implementer providing meaningful text alternatives (e.g., alt text, aria-labels).

standard: 1.2.3 Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded) (Level A)

observations: Compliance depends on the implementer structuring the page correctly (e.g., proper heading levels, logical grouping, label associations).
Compliance depends on the implementer providing appropriate autocomplete attributes where user data is collected.
Compliance depends on the implementer ensuring color is not the only visual means of conveying information.
Contrast meets the minimum 4.5:1.
Text is still visible and readable when resized to at least 200%.
Compliance depends on the implementer avoiding the use of images of text unless essential.
All user interface elements, as well as their states (focus, hover, select, check, visited/unvisited, expand/collapse and outline) have a colour contrast of 3:1.
Text is visible/readable when the line spacing is at least 1.5 times the font size, letter spacing is at least 0.12 times the font size, and word spacing is at least 0.16 times the font size.

standard: 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus (Level AA)

observations: Elements are accessible using a keyboard only.

standard: 2.3.1 Three Flashes or Below Threshold (Level A)

observations: Compliance depends on the implementer providing clear and descriptive link text.
Compliance depends on the implementer providing clear and descriptive headings and labels.
Focus is visible when navigating using a keyboard.
Compliance depends on the implementer ensuring any accessible name (e.g., aria-label) contains the visible text.
Compliance depends on the implementer setting the correct lang attribute on the html element.
Compliance depends on the implementer setting lang attributes on parts of the page that differ from the main language.
Compliance depends on the implementer ensuring that focusing an element does not trigger unexpected context changes.
Compliance depends on the implementer ensuring that interacting with a component (like inputting data) does not cause unexpected context changes.
Where sliders are used as part of the navigation this is consistent across the website.
Sliders are labelled consistently.
Compliance depends on the implementer providing clear error identification in the UI and handling validation logic.
Compliance depends on the implementer providing sufficient labels and instructions for user input.
Compliance depends on the implementer providing helpful suggestions when errors are detected.
While the component provides roles and states, compliance often depends on the implementer providing accessible names (e.g., for icon-only buttons).
Compliance depends on the implementer correctly exposing dynamic state changes to assistive technologies via aria-live regions.
